steps.title: Fastest path: divide population by area
steps.content: For population density, use total population divided by total area. The units in the question tell you to compute beavers per acre.

steps.title: Set up the density formula
steps.content:
Population density equals total population divided by total area:
$$\text{Density} = \frac{\text{Population}}{\text{Area}} = \frac{2{,}448}{136} \text{ beavers per acre}$$

steps.title: Divide
steps.content:
$$\frac{2{,}448}{136} = 18$$
Check: $136 \times 18 = 136 \times 10 + 136 \times 8 = 1{,}360 + 1{,}088 = 2{,}448.$ ✓

The estimated population density is 18 beavers per acre, which is Choice A.
